successStories.title: From Corporate Job to ₹45 Lakh/Year with High-Density Dragon Fruit in Maharashtra
How a 2-Acre Barren Land in Gujarat Now Yields Premium Export-Quality Pitaya
Transforming Water-Scarce Rajasthan with Drip-Irrigated Dragon Fruit Orchards

technologies.name: Drone-Based Liquid Fertigation
technologies.desc: Autonomous payload drones that aerially spray water-soluble micronutrients directly onto the cactus canopy, reducing manual labor costs by 80% and preventing soil compaction.

technologies.name: AI-Powered Soil Moisture Sensors
technologies.desc: IoT probes planted near the root zone that automatically trigger the drip irrigation system only when the soil volumetric water content drops below 15%, saving thousands of liters of water.

technologies.name: Solar-Powered Cold Storage Micro-Chambers
technologies.desc: Off-grid, solar-powered chilling units placed directly on the farm. They rapidly cool freshly harvested dragon fruit to 10°C, doubling shelf life and preventing immediate post-harvest loss.

technologies.name: LED Photoperiod Extension Lighting
technologies.desc: Using specialized LED grow lights strung across the concrete trellises to trick the dragon fruit plants into flowering during the off-season, capturing premium winter market prices.

technologies.name: Blockchain Traceability QR Codes
technologies.desc: Printing unique, unalterable QR codes on each fruit box so premium retail buyers can verify the farm's organic pesticide records, instantly commanding a 20% higher market rate.

agronomy.soil: Sandy loam to clay loam with excellent drainage. pH 5.5 to 7.0.
agronomy.states: Gujarat, Maharashtra, Karnataka, Andhra Pradesh, West Bengal.
agronomy.bestClimate: Tropical and subtropical. Best temperature range: 20°C to 30°C. Needs 50-100cm annual rainfall.
agronomy.unsuitableClimate: Areas with extreme frost, waterlogging, or continuous temperatures above 40°C which cause severe sunburn.
agronomy.varieties: Red Flesh (Siam Red), White Flesh (Vietnam White), Yellow Pitaya.

agronomy.soil: Sandy loam to shallow soils, highly drought-tolerant. Best pH 6.0 to 8.5.
agronomy.bestClimate: Arid to semi-arid regions. Needs hot summers and mild winters. Tolerates up to 48°C.
agronomy.unsuitableClimate: Heavy waterlogged soils, continuous high humidity.
agronomy.varieties: Kashmiri Red Apple Ber, Thai Green Apple Ber, Umran.

agronomy.soil: Well-drained fertile loam soil. Extremely sensitive to waterlogging. pH 6.0 to 6.5.
agronomy.states: Tamil Nadu, Andhra Pradesh, Maharashtra, Gujarat, Kerala.
agronomy.bestClimate: Tropical. Temperature between 22°C to 26°C. High humidity.
agronomy.unsuitableClimate: Frost-prone areas and regions with heavy strong winds.

agronomy.soil: Deep, rich loamy soil entirely free from nematodes. Depth of 1 meter. pH 6.5 to 7.5.
agronomy.states: Maharashtra (Jalgaon), Gujarat, Tamil Nadu, Andhra Pradesh.
agronomy.bestClimate: Tropical humid climate. Needs abundant sunshine and heavy water supply.
agronomy.unsuitableClimate: Dry arid regions without irrigation, extreme cold winters.

agronomy.soil: Well-drained medium deep soils. Can tolerate mild salinity. pH 6.5 to 7.5.
agronomy.bestClimate: Semi-arid climate. Needs hot dry summers for fruit ripening and cool winters.
agronomy.unsuitableClimate: Humid zones with heavy continuous rainfall (causes fungal blight).

agronomy.soil: Deep, well-drained lateritic or alluvial soil. Avoid rocky subsoil. pH 5.5 to 7.5.
agronomy.states: Maharashtra, Andhra Pradesh, Uttar Pradesh, Karnataka, Gujarat.
agronomy.bestClimate: Tropical/Subtropical. Distinct dry period needed before flowering.
agronomy.unsuitableClimate: Areas with frequent unseasonal rains during flowering stage.
agronomy.varieties: Alphonso (Hapus), Kesar, Banganappalli, Mallika (Ideal for UHDP).

agronomy.soil: Sandy loam with high organic matter. Well drained. pH 5.5 to 6.5.
agronomy.bestClimate: Temperate to sub-tropical. Needs cool nights (10°C) for best fruiting.
agronomy.unsuitableClimate: Hot and humid coastal areas without climate control.
agronomy.varieties: Camarosa, Chandler, Sweet Charlie, Winter Dawn.

agronomy.soil: Extremely well-drained loamy soil. Cannot tolerate waterlogging. pH 5.5 to 7.0.
agronomy.bestClimate: Sub-tropical to tropical. Temp 12°C to 30°C. Protect from hot dry winds.
agronomy.unsuitableClimate: Heavy clay soils with poor drainage, frost-prone zones.

agronomy.soil: Light loamy and well-drained soils. Avoid very heavy clay. pH 6.0 to 8.0.
agronomy.bestClimate: Sub-tropical. Withstands both heat and cold but sensitive to severe frost.

agronomy.soil: Sandy to sandy-loam soils. Good drainage is essential. pH 6.0 to 7.0.
agronomy.states: Karnataka, Tamil Nadu, Andhra Pradesh, Punjab.
agronomy.bestClimate: Warm, sunny, dry climate. Needs heat for sugar development.
agronomy.unsuitableClimate: Frost zones, heavy rains during fruit ripening stage.
agronomy.varieties: Arka Muthu, Sugar Baby, Namdhari (NS 295), Kiran.

agronomy.soil: Fertile, well-draining sandy loam. Avoid heavy soils. pH 6.0 to 6.8.
agronomy.bestClimate: Warm and dry. Net houses protect from pests and stabilize temperature.
agronomy.unsuitableClimate: Unprotected open fields in heavy monsoon.
